zh.lifeSummary: 當Reginald de St Leger於大約1115年出生在英國素可瓦特林時，他的父親Geoffrey de St Leger31歲，母親Agnes Clare26歲。他於1138年與Helisende d'Eu在英國素可瓦特林結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1176年在英國素可享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Reginald de St Leger于大约1115年出生在英国素可瓦特林时，他的父亲Geoffrey de St Leger31岁，母亲Agnes Clare26岁。他于1138年与Helisende d'Eu在英国素可瓦特林结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1176年在英国素可享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Reginald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin, derived from Reginaldus, a Latinized form of Reynold influenced by Latin regina ‘queen’. The full form is now regarded as very formal, and bearers generally shorten it to Reg in ordinary usage.

en.lifeSummary: When Reginald de St Leger was born about 1115, in Wartling, Sussex, England, his father, Geoffrey de St Leger, was 31 and his mother, Agnes Clare, was 26. He married Helisende d'Eu in 1138, in Wartling, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1176, in Sussex, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reginald de St Leger est né environ 1115, à Wartling, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey de St Leger, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Agnes Clare, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Helisende d'Eu en 1138, à Wartling, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1176, à Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Reginald de St Leger um 1115 in Wartling, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de St Leger 31 und seine Mutter Agnes Clare 26. Er hat 1138 in Wartling, Sussex, England Helisende d'Eu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1176 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Reginald de St Leger è nato il circa 1115 nel Wartling, Sussex, England, il suo padre, Geoffrey de St Leger, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Clare, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Helisende d'Eu nel 1138, nel Wartling, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1176, nel Sussex, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Reginald de St Legerが約1115年にイギリススセックスワートリングで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de St Legerは31歳，母Agnes Clareは26歳であった。1138年にイギリススセックスワートリングでHelisende d'Euと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1176年にイギリススセックスで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Reginald de St Leger(이)가 대략 1115년 영국 수세스 워틀링에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de St Leger(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Clare(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1138년 영국 수세스 워틀링에서 Helisende d'Eu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1176년 영국 수세스에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reginald de St Leger nasceu aproximadamente 1115, em Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Geoffrey de St Leger, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Clare, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Helisende d'Eu em 1138, em Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1176, em Sussex, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reginald de St Leger родился около 1115 г. в местности вартлинг, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey de St Leger было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes Clare, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Helisende d'Eu в 1138 г. в местности вартлинг, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1176 г. в местности суссекс, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Reginald de St Leger nació aproximadamente 1115, en Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Geoffrey de St Leger, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Agnes Clare, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Helisende d'Eu en 1138, en Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1176, en Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
